votes.reason: The Python code constructs the expected output by filtering the input list for elements that occur exactly once, preserving their original order, which exactly matches the three conditions in the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by keeping only the elements that appear exactly once in the input list while preserving their original order.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by constructing the expected list of elements that appear exactly once in the input, preserving their original order, and checking if the output matches this expected list.

The Python spec computes the list of elements that appear exactly once in input, preserving their order. This matches the Coq spec: (1) every element in output appears exactly once in input, (2) every element appearing exactly once in input is in output, (3) relative order is preserved. The Python implementation using list comprehension with count==1 correctly captures all three conditions.

The Python spec computes the list of elements that appear exactly once in input, preserving their order. The Coq spec requires: (1) every output element exists uniquely in input, (2) every unique input element appears in output, (3) relative order is preserved. These conditions together define exactly the same list as the Python implementation produces - elements appearing exactly once, in their original order.

The Python spec computes the list of elements that appear exactly once in input, preserving their order. This matches the Coq spec: (1) every element in output appears exactly once in input, (2) every element appearing exactly once in input is in output, (3) relative order is preserved. The Python implementation using list comprehension with count==1 achieves exactly this behavior.
Both require output to be exactly the elements that appear exactly once in input, in the same order. The Python list comprehension computes precisely this.

Both define output as the subsequence of input elements that occur exactly once, preserving input order. The Coq spec enforces inclusion of all and only unique elements and order preservation, which uniquely determines the same list the Python code builds.

Python constructs the subsequence of input elements that occur exactly once, preserving input order, and checks output equals it. This matches the Coq spec’s requirements on content (exactly unique elements) and order.
